findings.evidence: End.one_def/mul_def are `:= rfl` (Endomorphism.lean:60,65); FundamentalGroupoid.comp_eq/id_eq_path_refl are `:= rfl` (Basic.lean:297,299); the groupoid instance sets `id x := ⟦Path.refl x.as⟧`, `comp := Quotient.trans` (Basic.lean:288-289); `toPath` is the identity coercion (FundamentalGroup.lean:64).
findings.fix: Prove via `rw`/`simp only` over `CategoryTheory.End.mul_def`/`End.one_def` and `FundamentalGroupoid.comp_eq`/`id_eq_path_refl` instead of `rfl` (e.g. `by rw [End.mul_def, FundamentalGroupoid.comp_eq]`).
findings.issue: `fundamentalGroup_toPath_mul` (line 39) and `fundamentalGroup_toPath_one` (line 30) close by bare `rfl`, depending on definitional equality across three wrappers: `End` monoid mul/one → category `≫`/`𝟙` → FundamentalGroupoid `id`/`comp` fields → `Path.Homotopic.Quotient.refl`/`trans`. Named lemmas span exactly these layers; the bare `rfl` breaks silently if any of those instances is refactored, whereas a rewrite through the named lemmas survives (their statements are stable even if their own proofs change).

summary: The composition convention is now stated as explicit, documented equations and downstream proofs route through them robustly. But the two foundational bridging lemmas still close by bare `rfl`, relying on definitional equality across the End/category/Quotient wrappers where named Mathlib lemmas exist.
